<img>ResearchHub JOURNAL</img>

motor neuron injury<sup>34</sup>. While DAO loss-of-function can have complex, context-dependent effects<sup>35</sup>, CNR-401’s anti-excitotoxic and antioxidant properties could mitigate potential risks, as evidenced by preserved motor function in vivo. CNR-401 also suppressed MXRA5, a regulator of extracellular matrix (ECM) remodeling that promotes MMP-9 (matrix metalloproteinase-9, an enzyme that breaks down ECM proteins) activation and blood–spinal cord barrier disruption<sup>36</sup>. This likely reflects modulation of the TGF-β1 pathway, which regulates ECM homeostasis<sup>37</sup>. Gene ontology enrichment confirmed ECM-related processes among the top pathways altered by CNR-401, suggesting that they may contribute to an improved structural integrity of neural tissues.

**Figure 6.** (A, B) GO biological process (BP) enrichment analysis of differentially expressed genes between pairwise comparisons of interest is indicated. (C, D) KEGG pathway enrichment analysis of differentially expressed genes in each experimental comparison. (A, C) BMAA-affected vs. control; (B, D) BMAA-affected vs. CNR-401-treated. In all graphs, bar length represents the number of genes associated with each enriched GO term or pathway, and color intensity reflects the adjusted p-value for enrichment significance.
